sourcePrompt: build a note-taking app for my classes
summary: Layered REST API architecture for note-taking using native Node.js HTTP modules, TypeScript, route/service/type separation, and Jest with service and HTTP coverage, kept public by default.

directories.purpose: Application source files and runtime entrypoints.
Generated test coverage for the starter project.
HTTP route handlers for health and resource endpoints.
Business logic kept separate from request parsing.
TypeScript interfaces for request and domain shapes.

files.purpose: Create the top-level request handler and register routes.
Start the HTTP server and expose a clean process entrypoint.
Resource-specific business logic and placeholder data handling.
Shared request and entity types for the primary resource.
Health endpoint used for smoke tests and validation.
Resource route definitions for the generated CRUD starter API.
Starter Jest tests for the resource service and routes.
Validation test that confirms the health endpoint is wired up.

moduleBoundaries.app: Compose the HTTP server, route registration, and shared request flow.
moduleBoundaries.routes: Translate HTTP requests into service calls and serialize HTTP responses.
moduleBoundaries.services: Own business rules and placeholder data operations without HTTP concerns.
moduleBoundaries.tests: Validate the generated endpoints and starter business logic.

healthEndpoint.purpose: Report service health for smoke tests and validation.

authentication.approach: No authentication middleware is scaffolded by default; the API remains public unless later requirements change.

crossCuttingConcerns.errorHandling: Return consistent JSON error payloads from route modules and keep service failures explicit for easier Jest assertions.
crossCuttingConcerns.configuration: Keep runtime configuration minimal and environment-driven so the starter project stays easy to run locally.
crossCuttingConcerns.persistence: Leave persistence as a later decision and keep generated services independent from any database library.

majorDecisions.title: Runtime And Language
majorDecisions.choice: Use Node.js with TypeScript for the generated backend starter.
majorDecisions.rationale: The spec carries the chosen runtime and language, and the PRD scopes the MVP to a Node.js/TypeScript backend starter.
alternatives: Use plain JavaScript for faster initial output.
Target another runtime such as Bun or Deno.

majorDecisions.title: HTTP Server Choice
majorDecisions.choice: Use native Node.js HTTP modules behind route modules instead of introducing a larger framework at this stage.
majorDecisions.rationale: The server framework is now chosen in the spec, and this default keeps the generated starter lightweight while still supporting REST endpoints.
alternatives: Use Express for a more familiar middleware ecosystem.
Use Fastify for higher performance and plugin support.

majorDecisions.title: Module Layout
majorDecisions.choice: Adopt route/service/type separation, with optional middleware when authentication is required.
majorDecisions.rationale: The module layout comes from the spec and matches the PRD requirement for a clear modular scaffold.
alternatives: Keep everything in a single entrypoint for maximum simplicity.

majorDecisions.title: Persistence Boundary
majorDecisions.choice: Leave persistence as a later decision and keep generated services independent from any database library.
majorDecisions.rationale: The persistence strategy comes from the spec, and database integration is explicitly out of scope for the MVP.
alternatives: Add a repository layer and in-memory implementation immediately.
Commit to a database and ORM from the start.

majorDecisions.title: Testing Strategy
majorDecisions.choice: Generate Jest tests for service and HTTP coverage.
majorDecisions.rationale: The test framework and test scope now live in the spec, and the PRD requires starter test generation for the produced backend.
alternatives: Use `node:test` to avoid a testing dependency.
Limit tests to service-only unit coverage.

majorDecisions.title: Authentication Boundary
majorDecisions.choice: Keep the MVP starter public by default and avoid scaffolding unused auth middleware.
majorDecisions.rationale: The prompt does not require authentication, so the smaller architecture keeps the scaffold focused on the core resource API.
alternatives: Always scaffold auth middleware for future use.
Add a full auth subsystem preemptively.

implementationNotes: The scaffold stage should create the planned directories and starter files exactly as listed in `scaffoldPlan`.
The starter code stage should populate `src/app.ts` and `src/server.ts` first, then add the resource route and service modules.
Resource endpoints should follow the `health-and-resource-crud` pattern and use `/note-takings` as the default collection path.
Generated Jest tests should cover the service-and-http scope from the spec.
Authentication should remain an open extension point rather than introducing auth-specific files later in the pipeline.
openQuestions: Which data persistence approach should the generated project assume?
Are there any required endpoints beyond the default REST starter routes?
Does the API need authentication or can it be public?
